# IBM Diagrams

> IBM Diagrams is a Python package that generates professional architecture diagrams following the IBM Design Language - Technical Diagrams Guideline. Transform your infrastructure into beautiful, standards-compliant diagrams through Diagram as Code, Terraform integration, or JSON input.

## Getting Started

- [Overview](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams): Introduction to IBM Diagrams
- [Quick Start](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams#-quick-start): Your first diagram in 30 seconds
- [Installation](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/setup.md): Setup with uv or pip
- [Getting Started Guide](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/GETTING_STARTED.md): Complete setup and first steps

## Core Concepts

- [Diagram as Code](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md): Python API for programmatic diagram creation
- [Terraform Integration](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md): Auto-generate diagrams from .tfstate files
- [MCP Integration](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/mcp.md): Model Context Protocol server for AI assistants
- [Testing Guide](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/testing.md): Visual regression testing

## Design Standards

- [IBM Design Language](https://www.ibm.com/design/language/infographics/technical-diagrams/design): Technical diagrams guideline
- [IBM Color Palette](https://www.ibm.com/design/language/color): Color usage (Cyan, Teal, Purple, Magenta)
- [IBM Plex Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/?query=Plex): Typography (IBM Plex Sans and variants)
- [Shape Types](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#shape-types): Groups, Zones, Nodes, Actors

## Input Methods

### 1. Diagram as Code (Python)
Write Python code to create diagrams programmatically. Perfect for version control, rapid iteration, and reusable templates.

```python
from ibmdiagrams.ibmcloud.diagram import Diagram
from ibmdiagrams.ibmcloud.groups import IBMCloud, Region, VPC, Zone, Subnet
from ibmdiagrams.ibmcloud.compute import VirtualServer

with Diagram("my-architecture"):
    with IBMCloud("IBM Cloud"):
        with Region("Dallas"):
            with VPC("Production VPC"):
                with Zone("Zone 1", "10.10.0.0/18"):
                    with Subnet("App Subnet", "10.10.10.0/24"):
                        vsi = VirtualServer("Web Server", "10.10.10.4")
```

### 2. Terraform State Files
Automatically generate diagrams from existing infrastructure. Perfect for documentation, compliance audits, and onboarding.

```bash
# With custom labels (resource names, IPs)
ibmdiagrams cloud.tfstate

# With general labels (resource types only)
ibmdiagrams --general cloud.tfstate

# Generate Python code for customization
ibmdiagrams --python cloud.tfstate
```

### 3. JSON Input
Generate diagrams from internal JSON format (for advanced integrations).

```bash
ibmdiagrams cloud.json
```

## API Reference

### Diagram Class
Entry point for all diagrams. Controls output format, direction, and styling.

- [Diagram](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#diagram): Root container with configuration options
- Parameters: name, filename, output, direction, font

### Groups (Containers)
Represent "deployedOn" relationships. Act as containers for other resources.

- [IBMCloud](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Top-level IBM Cloud container
- [Region](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Geographic region
- [VPC](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Virtual Private Cloud
- [Zone](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Availability zone
- [Subnet](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Network subnet
- [SecurityGroup](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Security group
- [ResourceGroup](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Resource group
- [CloudServices](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#core-groups): Cloud services container

### Compute Resources
Virtual machines, bare metal servers, and compute services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources) for VirtualServer and PowerVirtualServer.

- [VirtualServer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): VPC virtual server instance (usage example provided)
- [PowerVirtualServer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): Power virtual server (usage example provided)
- [BareMetalServer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): Bare metal server
- [ClassicVirtualServer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): Classic virtual server
- [DedicatedHost](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): Dedicated host
- [ImageService](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): Image service
- [Satellite](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#compute-resources): IBM Cloud Satellite

### Network Components
Load balancers, gateways, and network services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components) for PublicGateway, ApplicationLoadBalancer, and TransitGateway.

- [PublicGateway](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Public gateway (usage example provided)
- [ApplicationLoadBalancer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Application load balancer (usage example provided)
- [TransitGateway](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Transit gateway (usage example provided)
- [LoadBalancer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Load balancer
- [NetworkLoadBalancer](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Network load balancer
- [VPNGateway](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): VPN gateway
- [EndpointGateway](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Virtual private endpoint gateway
- [DirectLinkDedicated](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Direct Link dedicated
- [DirectLinkConnect](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Direct Link connect
- [DNSServices](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): DNS services
- [FloatingIP](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#network-components): Floating IP address

### Storage Services
Object storage, block storage, and backup services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services) for ObjectStorage and BlockStorage.

- [ObjectStorage](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services): Cloud Object Storage (usage example provided)
- [BlockStorage](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services): Block Storage volumes (usage example provided)
- [BlockStorageSnapshots](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services): Block Storage snapshots
- [CloudBackup](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services): Cloud Backup service
- [FileStorage](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#storage-services): File Storage

### Security Services
Encryption, secrets management, and security services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services) for KeyProtect, SecretsManager, and VPNGateway.

- [KeyProtect](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): Key Protect encryption (usage example provided)
- [SecretsManager](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): Secrets Manager (usage example provided)
- [VPNGateway](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): VPN gateway (usage example provided)
- [SecurityComplianceCenter](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): Security & Compliance Center
- [AppID](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): App ID authentication
- [BastionHost](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): Bastion host
- [VPNConnection](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#security-services): VPN connection

### Observability
Monitoring, logging, and observability services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#observability) for all three services.

- [CloudLogs](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#observability): Cloud Logs (usage example provided)
- [FlowLogs](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#observability): VPC Flow Logs (usage example provided)
- [Monitoring](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#observability): IBM Cloud Monitoring (usage example provided)

### Data Services
Databases and data management services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services) for PostgreSQL and Redis.

- [PostgreSQL](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): PostgreSQL database (usage example provided)
- [Redis](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): Redis cache (usage example provided)
- [DB2](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): IBM Db2 database
- [DB2Warehouse](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): Db2 Warehouse
- [MySQL](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): MySQL database
- [Cloudant](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): Cloudant NoSQL database
- [EventStreams](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): Event Streams (Kafka)
- [Elasticsearch](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#data-services): Elasticsearch (not in import list)

### Containers & Kubernetes
Container orchestration and management services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#containers--kubernetes) for KubernetesService and OpenShift.

- [KubernetesService](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#containers--kubernetes): IBM Kubernetes Service (usage example provided)
- [OpenShift](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#containers--kubernetes): Red Hat OpenShift (usage example provided)
- [CodeEngine](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#containers--kubernetes): IBM Code Engine
- [ContainerRegistry](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#containers--kubernetes): Container Registry

### AI & Watson
AI, machine learning, and Watson services. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson) for WatsonX and WatsonAssistant.

- [WatsonX](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): watsonx platform (usage example provided)
- [WatsonAssistant](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): Watson Assistant (usage example provided)
- [WatsonXAI](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): watsonx.ai
- [WatsonXData](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): watsonx.data
- [WatsonXGovernance](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): watsonx.governance
- [WatsonDiscovery](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): Watson Discovery
- [WatsonStudio](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): Watson Studio (not in import list)
- [WatsonMachineLearning](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#ai--watson): Watson Machine Learning (not in import list)

### DevOps Services
CI/CD, source control, and DevOps tools. Note: DevOps services are available in the codebase but not documented in diagram-as-code.md. See [Terraform documentation](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#continuous-delivery) for Terraform resource support.

- ContinuousDelivery: Continuous Delivery
- ContinuousIntegration: Continuous Integration
- Toolchain: DevOps Toolchain
- SourceCodeRepository: Source code repository
- GitLab: GitLab
- Ansible: Ansible automation

### Actors
Users, applications, and external entities. See [usage examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#actors) for User and Application.

- [User](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#actors): Single user (usage example provided)
- [Application](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#actors): Application component (usage example provided)
- [Users](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#actors): Multiple users
- [Microservice](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#actors): Microservice

### Connectors
Visual connections between components.

- [SolidEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Solid line connection
- [PrivateSolidEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Private solid line
- [PublicSolidEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Public solid line
- [DashedEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Dashed line
- [DottedEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Dotted line
- [DoubleEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Double line
- [TunnelEdge](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#connectors): Tunnel line

## Examples

- [Simple VPC](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/examples/python/simple-vpc.md): Basic VPC with virtual server
- [VSI on VPC Landing Zone](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/examples/python/slzvsi.md): Enterprise landing zone with multi-zone deployment
- [Power Virtual Server](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/examples/python/slzpowervs.md): Power Systems infrastructure
- [Terraform Examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/docs/examples/terraform): Auto-generated from Terraform state
- [JSON Examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/docs/examples/json): JSON input format examples

## CLI Reference

### Basic Commands

```bash
# Generate diagram from Terraform state (custom labels)
ibmdiagrams cloud.tfstate

# Generate with general labels (resource types only)
ibmdiagrams --general cloud.tfstate

# Generate Python code instead of DrawIO
ibmdiagrams --python cloud.tfstate

# Specify output directory
ibmdiagrams -output ./diagrams cloud.tfstate

# Use custom font
ibmdiagrams -font "IBM Plex Sans JP" cloud.tfstate

# Show version
ibmdiagrams --version

# Show help
ibmdiagrams --help

# Start MCP server
ibmdiagrams --mcp
```

### Font Options

- `IBM Plex Sans` (default) - Latin script
- `IBM Plex Sans Arabic` - Arabic script
- `IBM Plex Sans Devanagari` - Devanagari script
- `IBM Plex Sans Hebrew` - Hebrew script
- `IBM Plex Sans JP` - Japanese script
- `IBM Plex Sans KR` - Korean script
- `IBM Plex Sans Thai` - Thai script

## MCP Server

IBM Diagrams provides a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for AI assistant integration.

### Configuration

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ibmdiagrams": {
      "command": "ibmdiagrams",
      "args": ["--mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Available Tools

- [generate_from_file](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/mcp.md#1-generate_from_file): Generate diagram from .tfstate or .json file
- [server_info](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/mcp.md#2-server_info): Get MCP server metadata

### Tool Parameters

**generate_from_file:**
- `inputfile` (required): Path to .tfstate or .json file
- `outputfolder` (optional): Output directory (default: current directory)
- `labeltype` (optional): "CUSTOM" or "GENERAL" (default: "CUSTOM")
- `codetype` (optional): "DRAWIO" or "PYTHON" (default: "DRAWIO")
- `fontname` (optional): Font family (default: "IBM Plex Sans")

## Source Code — IBM/ibmdiagrams

> Python package for generating IBM-standard architecture diagrams from code, Terraform, or JSON.

- [Repository](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams): Main repository
- [Core Module (ibmbase)](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/src/ibmdiagrams/ibmbase): Diagram generation engine
- [IBM Cloud Module (ibmcloud)](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/src/ibmdiagrams/ibmcloud): IBM Cloud components
- [Shapes Module (ibmshapes)](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/src/ibmdiagrams/ibmshapes): Alternative shape library
- [Scripts Module (ibmscripts)](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/src/ibmdiagrams/ibmscripts): CLI and MCP server
- [Tests](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/tests): Visual regression tests
- [Examples](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/tree/main/docs/examples): Python, Terraform, and JSON examples

## Best Practices

### Diagram as Code

- [Use Meaningful Names](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#1-use-meaningful-names): Descriptive resource names
- [Use Sublabels](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#2-use-sublabels-for-technical-details): Technical details in sublabels
- [Choose Direction](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#3-choose-appropriate-direction): LR for wide, TB for deep hierarchies
- [Group Resources](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#4-group-related-resources): Use ResourceGroup or LayoutGroup
- [Keep It Simple](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#5-keep-it-simple): Flatten where possible
- [Use Variables](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#6-use-variables-for-reusable-components): Reusable components

### Terraform Integration

- [Descriptive Names](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#1-use-descriptive-resource-names): Use clear resource names
- [Add Tags](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#2-add-tags-for-better-organization): Organize with tags
- [Naming Conventions](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#3-use-consistent-naming-conventions): Consistent patterns
- [CI/CD Integration](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#4-generate-diagrams-in-cicd): Automate diagram generation
- [Secure State Files](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#5-keep-state-files-secure): Don't commit state files
- [Document Generation](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/terraform.md#6-document-diagram-generation): Document the process

## Known Limitations

- [Group Spanning](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/docs/diagram-as-code.md#group-spanning): Cannot represent groups spanning other groups
- [Connectors](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams#%EF%B8%8F-known-limitations): Currently direct point-to-point connections

## Resources

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams): Source code and issues
- [Releases](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/releases): Download wheel files
- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines
- [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Community standards
- [Security Policy](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Security reporting
- [Changelog](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history
- [License](https://github.com/IBM/ibmdiagrams/blob/main/LICENSE): Apache 2.0 license

## External Resources

- [IBM Design Language](https://www.ibm.com/design/language/infographics/technical-diagrams/design): Technical diagrams guideline
- [draw.io Desktop](https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop/releases): Diagram editor
- [IBM Plex Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/?query=Plex): Typography downloads
- [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/): MCP documentation
- [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp): MCP framework
- [uv Package Manager](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv): Fast Python package manager
